name: Lamb Couscous
recipeIngredient: 1 Cucumber (topped and sliced, seeded and diced)
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly saute the cubed lamb in 2 tablespoons of olive oil until browned. Remove the lamb and set aside.
2. Add the onions to the pan and cook over low heat for about 10 minutes, without coloring.
3. Stir in the minced garlic and saute for 1 minute, then return the lamb to the pan.
4. Add the saffron threads and pour in the stock. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Bring to a simmer and cover. Cook for one hour, or until the lamb is tender. If there is excess liquid, remove the meat and onions and reduce the stock over high heat.
6. Rinse the couscous under cold water, then add boiling water. Cover with a tea towel and let stand for 10 minutes, or until all the water is absorbed.
7. Fluff the couscous with a fork. Stir in the chopped spring onion, diced red pepper, sliced cucumber, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
8. Add the chopped herbs (mint, chives, parsley, coriander) and keep warm.
9. To serve, place spoonfuls of lamb on top of the couscous and serve with plenty of juice.
